title: Finite formal metric jets
summary: Defines finite component-level ρ-jets of g_ρ and packages the first Fefferman–Graham condition g⁽¹⁾ = 2P as an explicit hypothesis.

mathematicalContent.heading: Finite ρ-jet
mathematicalContent.body: A FormalMetricJet stores symmetric-two-tensor-shaped coefficient functions g⁽ᵐ⁾ for 0 ≤ m ≤ K, together with a base metric g and a Schouten-shaped tensor P. Only the zeroth equality g⁽⁰⁾ = g is built into the jet structure.

mathematicalContent.heading: First FG condition
mathematicalContent.body: SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder stores the order bound and, whenever the requested order is at least one, the relation g⁽¹⁾ = 2P. FG_first_jet extracts this stored relation at order one; it is not derived here from the Ricci equations.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A component-level covariant two-tensor is represented simply as a real-valued function of two base indices.
This predicate says that the two-index component function is symmetric.
A K-jet stores coefficients g⁽⁰⁾,…,g⁽ᴷ⁾, a base metric g, a Schouten tensor placeholder P, and the equality g⁽⁰⁾ = g.
Given m ≤ K, coeffAt accesses the m-th stored jet coefficient.
correspondence.leanType: def coeffAt (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (m : ℕ) (hm : m ≤ K) : Sym2Component ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zeroth coefficient accessed through coeffAt is the stored base metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem coeffAt_zero (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) : J.coeffAt 0 (Nat.zero_le K) = J.baseMetric

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: When K ≥ 1, firstCoeff is the stored coefficient g⁽¹⁾.
correspondence.leanType: def firstCoeff (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (hK : 1 ≤ K) : Sym2Component ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This proposition-valued structure records order ≤ K and takes the first-jet identity g⁽¹⁾ = 2P as a field whenever order ≥ 1.
correspondence.leanType: structure SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (order : ℕ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If the stored FG conditions hold to order one, the first coefficient equals twice the stored Schouten tensor.
correspondence.leanType: theorem FG_first_jet (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (H : SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder J 1) (i j : ι) : J.coeffAt 1 H.order_le i j = 2 * J.schouten i j

references.locator: Chapter 3, formal ρ-expansion and the first normal-form coefficient
references.note: Source for the formal metric expansion and the relation g′|_{ρ=0}=2P.

limitations: The coefficients are arbitrary component functions and are not required to be symmetric. SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder stores only an order bound and the first-jet relation; it does not encode the full Ricci equations at higher orders or derive g⁽¹⁾ = 2P.
